prompt: We're planning a zero-downtime migration from DynamoDB to PostgreSQL. Before committing, I need to shadow-run all production DynamoDB queries against a PostgreSQL replica to measure correctness and latency differences. Design the ghost observation setup including what it can and cannot prove.

Our ML model serves predictions in production. We want to A/B test a new model version but first need a shadow mode where the new model scores every request without its predictions reaching users. The comparison should measure latency, accuracy, and edge case behavior.

A customer reported intermittent data corruption. I need to observe the exact sequence of database transactions during their workflow without adding overhead that would change the timing and potentially mask the race condition. Design the observation strategy.

We're evaluating whether to move from a message queue (RabbitMQ) to an event stream (Kafka). Design a ghost tap that mirrors all RabbitMQ messages to a Kafka topic so we can validate consumer behavior, throughput, and ordering guarantees without touching the production message flow.

Legal wants to understand what personally identifiable information flows through our system during a typical user session — but they don't want us to create a new data store that itself becomes a liability. Design an ephemeral observation that maps PII flow without persisting it.

Before updating our Kubernetes network policies, I need to see which pods currently communicate with which others. Map the actual network flow graph without injecting a service mesh or modifying any deployments. What's visible in ghost mode and what requires real instrumentation?

We suspect our third-party payment processor is adding variable latency. I want to observe the exact timing of every payment API call in production over 48 hours without adding middleware that could itself cause latency. Design the passive observation approach.

Our multi-tenant system may have a noisy neighbor problem. I need to observe per-tenant resource usage across CPU, memory, network, and disk I/O without the observation itself becoming a noisy neighbor. Design the lightweight ghost observation with explicit caveats on accuracy.
